technique: IOC enrichment - reputation of an attacking client IP on open threat-intel feeds

trigger.from_breadth: An IIS / APP finding with an A03-injection / webshell / traversal / SSRF / scanner token in URI_Query (or the raw W3SVC message) AND an EXTERNAL client IP. This is the 'exploitation in URI_Query -> check the Client_ip on a threat feed' enrichment.

telemetry_required: Client_ip (OP-GL parses it; PROD/AZ/DEV frequently NULL -> take the source IP from the raw W3SVC message, or the Origin_ip enrichment field)
at least one TI API key in .ti-keys.json (virustotal / abuseipdb / otx)
telemetry_gap_behavior: If Client_ip is null, extract the requesting IP from the raw message before enriching. If NO TI key is configured, ti-lookup returns verdict=no-data -> STILL report the exploitation finding, just without reputation (a missing feed never makes an attack clean). If the IP is RFC1918/loopback or allow-listed, note internal/allow-listed and skip the feed.

inputs.entity_ip: <external client IP from the exploitation finding>

method.goal: Extract the external client IP from the exploitation finding
method.compute: Client_ip on OP-GL; on PROD/AZ/DEV parse the IP from the raw W3SVC line or use Origin_ip. Skip if RFC1918/loopback (internal) or on the Cato/Nessus allow-list.

method.goal: Query the threat-intel feeds (deterministic, cached, 0 model tokens)
method.compute: parse the JSON verdict: verdict (malicious|suspicious|clean|no-data|internal|allowlisted), score, virustotal.malicious (vendor count), abuseipdb.confidence (0-100), otx.pulses, as_owner, country, sources_checked

method.goal: Correlate the IP across the rest of this run (entity pivot)
method.compute: does the same IP appear in other surface findings this window? hosting/VPS/Tor as_owner, or a country outside the GEO-ACL, raises severity
method.decision_input: same IP attacking multiple surfaces + a TI-malicious verdict = coordinated actor -> escalate the whole chain

decision.CRITICAL_conf5: TI verdict malicious AND the exploitation request got HTTP 2xx (confirmed compromise from a known-bad IP)
decision.HIGH_conf4: TI verdict malicious on the attacking IP even where the request was blocked (403/404/302) - a known-bad actor is actively targeting; OR TI suspicious + HTTP 2xx
decision.MEDIUM_conf3: exploitation token present but TI verdict clean/no-data - report the ATTEMPT (a clean reputation does NOT exonerate a logged attack)
decision.FP_kill: the IP is a sanctioned scanner (Nessus/Tenable) or Cato egress; a single stale OTX pulse on a recycled CDN/hosting IP with no VT/AbuseIPDB corroboration; legitimate security-research scanners you have whitelisted

false_positive_checks: Cato egress 140.82.202.196 / 199.27.40.187 / 123.253.153.138 and Nessus/Tenable 10.102.100.199 / 10.101.100.160 / 10.250.100.77 / 10.220.100.199 -> ti-lookup returns allowlisted/internal; never escalate
shared CDN / cloud-hosting IP (Cloudflare, AWS, Azure) with a low VT count can be a neighbor FP -> weigh as_owner + require AbuseIPDB or multiple VT vendors
one old OTX pulse alone (pulses < 3) is weak -> treat as suspicious not malicious unless VT/AbuseIPDB agree

output_findings_json.query_template: <the IIS exploitation query that fired> ; enrich anchor_ip via ti-lookup.ps1 -Ip <Client_ip>
output_findings_json.ti: {verdict, score, vt_malicious, abuseipdb_confidence, otx_pulses, as_owner, country}
